---
title: "Running tests"
description: "Fetch test data from the repo's bucket, then run component and workflow tests."
order: 40
---

Components and workflows are tested with Viash. The **test mechanics are Viash's**
— see the [Viash guide](https://viash.io/guide/) for writing tests. This page
covers what is specific to OpenPipeline: where the test data comes from and the
workflow integration-test convention.

## Test data comes from the bucket

Test data is **not stored in the repository** — it lives in a remote bucket, and
each package repository declares its own in its `_viash.yaml` (you don't edit
that). Rather than duplicating the download logic, the core `openpipeline`
package provides the `sync_test_resources` component. From the **root of the repo
you're working in**, run it straight from `openpipeline` with Nextflow — it reads
the local `_viash.yaml` and syncs into `resources_test/`:

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  -r 4.2.0 -latest -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/download/sync_test_resources/main.nf \
  --input _viash.yaml \
  --output .
```

Run it from the repo you're working in so it picks up that repo's `_viash.yaml`
(and thus its bucket) — don't reuse another package's data. Use the `openpipeline`
version your package pins.

## Running component tests

`viash test` builds the component on the fly and runs its test:

```bash
viash test src/<namespace>/<component>/config.vsh.yaml   # one component
viash ns test --parallel -q <namespace>                  # a whole namespace
viash ns test --parallel                                 # the whole suite
```

## Running workflow tests

Each workflow ships with a `nextflow.config` (needed to run it) and a `test.nf`
that `viash test` executes. On top of that, OpenPipeline workflows carry an
**`integration_test.sh`** by convention — a script that runs the workflow
end-to-end against the test data, for local integration testing:

```bash
bash src/workflows/<category>/<workflow>/integration_test.sh
```

:::{.callout-note}
All of these need the repository's data present in `resources_test/` — run
`sync_test_resources` first.
:::
